name: search-enhancer
description: Enhanced code search with semantic understanding, pattern matching, and intelligent query interpr...
capabilities: Translates natural language queries into search commands
Provides regex patterns for JavaScript, Python, Go, and TypeScript
Suggests filtering by file type and directory scope
Teaches cross-reference and dependency tracking searches
Identifies security and performance anti-patterns via search
Optimizes search performance for large codebases
author: CuriousLearner
securitySummary: This is a pure documentation skill containing only markdown text with example shell commands. All static findings are false positives triggered by example code patterns in documentation. No executable code exists. The skill teaches developers how to use grep and ripgrep for code search.
valueStatement: Finding code in large projects wastes valuable development time. This skill teaches you advanced search techniques with grep and ripgrep for instant code discovery.
userTitle: Find Code Faster with Intelligent Search Patterns
limitations: Requires grep or ripgrep to be installed on your system
Cannot search inside binary or compiled files
Regex patterns may need adjustment for edge cases
Does not replace IDE-integrated semantic analysis tools

useCases.title: Find API Endpoints Quickly
useCases.description: Locate all Express, Flask, or Go HTTP handlers using smart regex patterns across your codebase

useCases.title: Audit for Hardcoded Secrets
useCases.description: Search for API keys, passwords, and tokens that should not be in source code

useCases.title: Track Technical Debt
useCases.description: Find all TODO comments, deprecated APIs, and code quality issues for sprint planning

promptTemplates.title: Find Functions
promptTemplates.prompt: Search for all async functions in the src directory

promptTemplates.title: Security Audit
promptTemplates.prompt: Find all places where SQL queries use string concatenation

promptTemplates.title: React Hook Usage
promptTemplates.prompt: Find all components using useState and useEffect hooks

promptTemplates.title: Cross-Reference Analysis
promptTemplates.prompt: Find all files that import and use the AuthService class

bestPractices: Always exclude node_modules, dist, and build directories from searches
Use ripgrep for speed and automatic gitignore respect
Start with broad searches and add filters progressively
antiPatterns: Searching entire codebase without file type or directory filters
Using case-sensitive search when matching identifiers of any case
Including generated files and dependencies in search results

faq.question: Which search tool is recommended?
faq.answer: Ripgrep (rg) is fastest and respects .gitignore automatically. Use grep when ripgrep is not available.

faq.question: How do I search across multiple lines?
faq.answer: Use ripgrep with -U flag for multiline mode: rg -U 'pattern.*\n.*continuation'

faq.question: Can I search for patterns in specific file types?
faq.answer: Yes, use -t flag with ripgrep: rg 'pattern' -t js -t ts or --glob with grep.

faq.question: How do I exclude test files from results?
What languages does this skill support?
faq.answer: The skill provides patterns for JavaScript, TypeScript, Python, Go, and any text-based language.

faq.question: How can I count matches instead of showing them?
faq.answer: Use --count flag with ripgrep: rg 'TODO' --count shows match counts per file.
